LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DOE DEFENDANT ONE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS MADE A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT THE ALLEGED CONDUCT ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY UNDER THE STATUTE. PLAINTIFF HAS PRODUCED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO DEMONSTRATE A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF HAS ALSO DEMONSTRATED A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TORTIOUS INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE. THE COURT RULED ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ON THE RECORD. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF WINELAND -THOMPSON ADVENTURES, INC'S MOTION FOR ORDER OF COTNEMPT AND SANCTIONS AGAINST FAILURE TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO SUBPOENA IS OFF CALENDAR. DISCOVERY IS STAYED IN LIGHT OF DEFENDANTS' FILING OF THE SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. (CCP 425.16(G).) THE STAY ON "[A]LL DISCOVERY PROCEEDINGS" APPLIES TO DISCOVERY MOTIONS, "INCLUDING THOSE ALREADY PENDING AT THE TIME THE SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS FILED." (BRITTS V. SUP.CT. (2006) 145 CAL.APP.4TH 1112, 1128.) PLAINTIFF MAY RE-FILE ITS MOTION AFTER THE RULING ON THE SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Defendant Doe Defendant One'S Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARTIN GARBUS, TEL. NO. (212) 561-3625 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LAURIE E. SHERWOOD OF THE LAW FIRM OF WALSWORTH FRANKLIN & MCCALL, LLP, TEL. NO. 781-7072 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSHUA KOLTUN, TEL. NO. 680-3410 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Doe Defendant One'S Motion To Strike Complaint is denied. Defendant has made a prima facie showing that the alleged conduct arises from protected activity under the statute. Plaintiff has produced sufficient evidence to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the original complaint. Plaintiff has also demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the second cause of action for tortious interference with prospective economic advantage. The court ruled on the evidentiary objections on the record. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

ATTY. JOSHUA KOLTUN, TEL. NO. 680-3410 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED WHICH SHE DID EARLIER IN THIS CASE, THAT HER FAMILY WENT ON A TRIP WITH THOMSON TRAVEL IN 2004 WHICH IS RELATED TO THE PLAINTIFF IN THIS CASE. THIS IS NOT A BASIS FOR HER RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. DEFENDANT DOE DEFENDANT ONE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 20, 2013 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion And Moton For Order Of Cotnempt And Sanctions Against Failure To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena
Off Calendar Jun-14-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOSHUA KOLTUN, ESQ., 415.680.3410, FOR DOE DEFENDANT ONE AND 3RD PARTY WEEBLY INC.; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF WINELAND -THOMPSON ADVENTURES, INC'S Motion For Order Of Cotnempt And Sanctions Against Failure To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena IS Off calendar. Discovery is stayed in light of Defendants' filing of the special motion to strike. (CCP 425.16(g).) The stay on "[a]ll discovery proceedings" applies to discovery motions, "including those already pending at the time the special motion to strike is filed." (Britts v. Sup.Ct. (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 1112, 1128.) Plaintiff may re-file its motion after the ruling on the special motion to strike. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Moton For Order Of Cotnempt And Sanctions Against Failure To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena
Off Calendar May-30-2013 Continued To Jun-14-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joshua Koltun, Esq. of the Law Office of Joshua Koltun, Tel. 415.680.3410, for defendant and third party witness;
No other appearance.

Before hearing, Judge Miller disclosed that in summer 2004 she travelled to Tanzania on a Thomson Family Travel trip. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in the case. Judge Miller has no personal knowledge of this case.

RULING: At the request of defendant's counsel, PLAINTIFF WINELAND-THOMPSON ADVENTURES, INC. DBA THOMSON'S Motion For Order Of Contempt And Sanctions Against Failure To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena is continued to June 14, 2013 at 9:30 am in Department 302 to enable counsel to discuss Judge Miller's disclosure with his client. Defendant's counsel shall give notice of the new hearing date. No further briefing is necessary. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
